seo.title: How Much Does Payroll Software Cost in Jamaica? (2026 Pricing Guide)
seo.description: A transparent breakdown of what payroll software costs for Jamaican businesses in 2026 — from free tools and spreadsheets to cloud payroll software and fully managed payroll outsourcing. Includes local JMD pricing.
keywords: how much does payroll software cost Jamaica
small business payroll software Jamaica price

sections.heading: What does payroll software cost in Jamaica in 2026?
sections.content: Most Jamaican business owners ask this question before they start looking for a payroll solution. The honest answer: it depends on how many employees you have, whether you want to run payroll yourself or fully outsource it, and whether you use software built for Jamaica or adapt a generic international tool. This guide breaks down every option with real JMD pricing.

sections.heading: Option 1 — Do it manually (J$0, but expensive in time and risk)
sections.content:
Many Jamaican small businesses calculate payroll in Excel or even by hand. The software cost is zero, but the hidden costs are significant:

- **Time**: 5–10 hours per payroll cycle for a business with 5–10 employees
- **Error risk**: PAYE thresholds change periodically; NIS ceilings update; manual calculations are prone to mistakes
- **TAJ penalties**: Under-remittance of PAYE or NIS triggers penalties and compound interest from Tax Administration Jamaica
- **Audit risk**: Manual records are harder to defend in a TAJ audit

For businesses with 1–2 employees and a consistent payroll, manual calculation is feasible — but the moment anything changes (new employee, salary adjustment, rate update), the risk compounds.

sections.heading: Option 2 — Cloud payroll software (J$2,500–J$15,000/month)
sections.content:
Cloud payroll software built for Jamaica is the sweet spot for most small and medium businesses. You run payroll yourself — the software handles all the calculations — and pay a monthly subscription that scales with your headcount.

**Payroll Jamaica pricing (2026):**
- Starter (up to 3 employees): J$2,500/month
- Small Business (up to 10 employees): J$5,500/month
- Growing (up to 25 employees): J$9,500/month
- Enterprise (26+ employees): Custom pricing

All plans include PAYE/NIS/NHT/Education Tax automation, payslip generation, TAJ remittance reports, and annual return prep (SO1, P24). No setup fees. No annual contract.

**What you get for J$2,500/month**: You run compliant payroll in minutes, not hours. The system auto-updates statutory rates when the Ministry of Finance announces changes. You never have to look up the current PAYE threshold again.

sections.heading: Option 3 — Managed payroll outsourcing (J$5,000–J$20,000/month)
sections.content:
If you want to hand off the entire payroll process to a team, managed payroll outsourcing is the answer. You provide the hours/salary data; the payroll company handles everything else.

**Payroll Jamaica managed service pricing:**
- Up to 5 employees: from J$5,000/month
- 6–15 employees: from J$9,000/month
- 16–30 employees: from J$15,000/month

This option is most cost-effective when: your business has no internal HR capacity, payroll is too complex to manage in-house (variable hours, seasonal staff, multiple pay grades), or the business owner's time is worth more than the outsourcing cost.

**What's included**: Every calculation, every payslip, every TAJ remittance, annual returns, and a dedicated payroll contact who knows your business.

sections.heading: Option 4 — Generic international software (US$10–US$80/month)
sections.content:
Several international payroll platforms (QuickBooks Payroll, Gusto, Paychex) operate in the Caribbean. They are not built for Jamaica and carry significant limitations:

- No automatic PAYE threshold updates for Jamaica
- No NIS, NHT, or Education Tax built in — you configure these manually as custom deductions
- TAJ remittance formats not supported
- Pricing in USD (at today's exchange rate: ~J$1,550–J$12,400/month before conversion fees)
- Support teams unfamiliar with Jamaica statutory requirements

For a Jamaican business, using a generic international platform typically costs more time (custom configuration, manual rate tracking) than switching to a Jamaica-specific solution, and at a similar or higher cost once you factor in exchange rates.

sections.heading: What drives the cost of payroll software in Jamaica?
sections.content:
**Number of employees**: All payroll software prices by headcount. A 3-person business will always pay significantly less than a 30-person business.

**Self-service vs. managed**: Running payroll yourself (software) is always cheaper than outsourcing. The outsourcing premium is typically 1.5–2x the software subscription cost.

**Pay frequency**: Weekly payroll cycles cost more to administer than monthly. Some providers charge a per-cycle fee on top of the subscription.

**Complexity**: Businesses with variable hours, commission structures, tip calculations, or multiple pay grades require more configuration and sometimes bump into higher price tiers.

**Local vs. international**: Jamaica-specific software avoids the overhead of manually maintaining statutory rates, custom deduction setups, and TAJ format workarounds.

sections.heading: Is payroll software worth it for a Jamaican small business?
sections.content:
For most Jamaican small businesses, yes — and the break-even point is typically within the first month.

If your time is worth J$1,500/hour (a conservative estimate for a business owner), and payroll software saves you 3 hours per month, the software pays for itself at J$4,500/month of time saved. Payroll Jamaica's Starter plan at J$2,500/month saves more than that for most users.

The stronger argument is risk reduction: a single TAJ penalty for late PAYE remittance can exceed an entire year's software subscription cost. Getting payroll right from the start protects your business from back-taxes, interest, and the stress of a TAJ audit.

sections.heading: How to choose the right payroll solution for your Jamaica business
sections.content:
**Choose software (self-service) if**: You have a consistent team, predictable payroll, and someone in-house (owner, accountant, or admin) with 1–2 hours to process payroll each cycle.

**Choose managed outsourcing if**: You want to eliminate payroll admin entirely, your payroll is complex (variable hours, seasonal staff), or you have no internal capacity for payroll processing.

**Must-haves for any Jamaica payroll solution**:
1. Built for Jamaica — not a generic international platform with manual workarounds
2. Automatic PAYE threshold and NIS rate updates
3. TAJ-compliant remittance report output
4. JMD pricing with no hidden USD conversion fees
5. Local support that understands Jamaica statutory requirements
